---
title: "ModelInfo"
---

## Overview

`dl::ModelInfo` ([`ModelInfo.hpp`](../../../../src/DeepLearning/models_v2/ModelInfo.hpp)) is a
torch-free struct that aggregates display and constraint metadata for a deep-learning
model. It bundles identity fields, input/output `TensorSlotDescriptor` vectors,
batch-size hints, and an optional post-encoder recommendation.

The type lives in `models_v2/` alongside
[`TensorSlotDescriptor.hpp`](../../../../src/DeepLearning/models_v2/TensorSlotDescriptor.hpp)
so UI, registry, and constraint code can query model metadata without including
libtorch headers.

## Struct

```cpp
struct ModelInfo {
    std::string model_id;
    std::string display_name;
    std::string description;
    std::vector<TensorSlotDescriptor> inputs;
    std::vector<TensorSlotDescriptor> outputs;
    int preferred_batch_size = 0;   // 0 = no preference
    int max_batch_size = 0;         // 0 = unlimited (legacy hint)
    BatchMode batch_mode = DynamicBatch{1, 0};
    std::string recommended_post_encoder;  // empty = none
};
```

| Field | Source | Notes |
|-------|--------|-------|
| `model_id`, `display_name`, `description` | `ModelBase` | Registry key and UI labels |
| `inputs` | `ModelBase::inputSlots()` | Raw model input schema |
| `outputs` | See below | May be raw or post-encoder-adjusted |
| `preferred_batch_size`, `max_batch_size` | `ModelBase` | Legacy hints; spinbox uses `preferred_batch_size` for dynamic models |
| `batch_mode` | `ModelBase::batchMode()` | Primary batch constraint; see [Batch Mode Guide](../batch_mode_guide.qmd) |
| `recommended_post_encoder` | `ModelBase::recommendedPostEncoderModule()` | Registry key (e.g. `"global_avg_pool"`); empty if none |

Each `TensorSlotDescriptor` describes one named tensor slot (shape excluding batch,
encoder/decoder hints, `is_static`, `sequence_dim`, `dtype`, etc.). See the
[Deep Learning overview](../index.qmd#model-layer-models_v2) for slot field summary.

## Registry vs live instance

Two query paths populate `ModelInfo` differently:

| API | When to use | `outputs` |
|-----|-------------|-----------|
| `ModelRegistry::getModelInfo(model_id)` | Model picker, initial slot layout before load | Raw `ModelBase::outputSlots()` |
| `SlotAssembler::currentModelInfo()` | Active session with weights loaded | `effectiveOutputSlots(raw_outputs, post_encoder_module)` |

`SlotAssembler::getModelInfo(model_id)` forwards to the registry.

`currentModelInfo()` returns `std::nullopt` when no model is loaded. When a
post-encoder module is active, only the **first** output slot descriptor is
shape-transformed (e.g. `[B,C,H,W]` → `[B,C]` after global average pool). This
matches what decoders see at inference time.

The Properties widget typically:

1. Loads registry `ModelInfo` when the user selects a model from the combo box.
2. Refreshes to `currentModelInfo()` after weights load, post-encoder sync, or
   configurable models (e.g. General Encoder) apply runtime shape settings.

## ModelRegistry cache

`ModelRegistry::getModelInfo()` is thread-safe and lazily populates an internal
`_info_cache` on first query per `model_id`:

1. Instantiate a temporary `ModelBase` via the registered factory.
2. Copy metadata fields into `ModelInfo`.
3. Store and return the cached copy.

Subsequent queries return the cache without reconstructing the model. `clear()`
wipes factories and cache (primarily for tests).

Runtime JSON models registered via `registerFromJson()` follow the same path:
a `RuntimeModel` factory is added and cached like any C++-registered model.

Slot lookup helpers read from the same cache:

- `getInputSlot(model_id, slot_name)`
- `getOutputSlot(model_id, slot_name)` — raw output slots only (no post-encoder)

## Consumers

| Consumer | Usage |
|----------|-------|
| `DeepLearningPropertiesWidget` | Model description, batch spinbox constraints, slot panel rebuild; holds `_current_info` refreshed from registry then live instance |
| `DeepLearningViewWidget` | Slot metadata for the view panel |
| `dl::constraints::computeBatchSizeConstraint` | Reads `batch_mode` with session memory frames |
| `ModelRegistry` | Internal cache; combo population via `availableModels()` |

## Related documentation

- [Batch Mode Guide](../batch_mode_guide.qmd) — `batch_mode` semantics and JSON mapping
- [Session Constraints](../constraints.qmd) — `computeBatchSizeConstraint()`
- [Post-encoder modules](../post_encoder_modules.qmd) — shape propagation into `effectiveOutputSlots()`

## Tests

- `tests/DeepLearning/registry/ModelRegistry.test.cpp` — `[ModelRegistry]` cache and field population
- `tests/DeepLearning/models_v2/NeuroSAMModel.test.cpp` — registry `ModelInfo` for NeuroSAM
- `tests/DeepLearning/constraints/ConstraintEnforcer.test.cpp` — `[dl_constraints]` with synthetic `ModelInfo`
